texto: You speak of the hand of Fatma, my friends, but you do not know the terrible power of this hand, the hand of monsters that menace you and seduce you with their subterranean access, their metallic splintering, their inhuman grotesqueness of idols.
explicacao: Citação de Georges Limbour sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “You speak of the hand of Fatma, my friends, but you do not know the terrible power of this hand, …” articula reflexao, monsters, speak em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.

texto: Once the frontiers of horror have been crossed, one will pass from form to form beyond the human and from metamorphosis to metamorphosis to accomplish, in the anguish of an impossible return, the most terrible journey to the depths of darkness.
explicacao: Citação de Georges Limbour sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Once the frontiers of horror have been crossed, one will pass from form to form beyond the human …” articula reflexao, horror, metamorphosis em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como viagem e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: Once the frontiers of horror have been crossed, one will pass from form to form beyond the human and from metamorphosis to metamorphosis to accomplish, in the anguish of an impossible return, the most terrible journey to the depths of darkness.

texto: The Actor, noticing a closed bookshop, dismounted from the horse which he tied to a street lamp. He woke up the bookseller and bought a Spanish grammar and dictionary. He set out again across town marveling at the way that the words of the foreign language were freshly gathered fruits and not old and dry. They touched the senses marvelously, new like young beggars who accost you, not yet words but the every things they designate, happily running naked before being clothed again in abstraction.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Georges Limbour: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “The Actor, noticing a closed bookshop, dismounted from the horse which he tied to a street lamp. …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, language, words pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de words.

texto: And there above all of these shops hung a blood soaked sign: a red hand, the hand of a child that was neither male nor female and yet roused feelings of the most dejected and criminal love
explicacao: Citação de Georges Limbour sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “And there above all of these shops hung a blood soaked sign: a red hand, the hand of a child that…” articula familia, crime, love em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como love.
